methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Fervo Energy is a pre-IPO climate and energy company founded by Tim Latimer and Jack Norbeck, having raised $1.5B+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a fragmented comp universe: one public peer (LNZA) and eight private companies in the Climate & Energy sector, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The peer-median method is not applicable here due to insufficient narrowly-scoped comparables. Of the 19 same-sector peers identified, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints in any single category—below the threshold required for reliable triangulation. A broad all-sector median would introduce substantial error given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and capital intensity within Climate & Energy.

Without sufficient narrow peer density, this analysis cannot produce a defensible valuation range using standard triangulation methods. The single public comp and small private peer set lack the consistency needed to establish credible EV/revenue or EV/EBITDA multiples specific to Fervo's stage and technology profile. Key uncertainties include the absence of operational or profitability data for Fervo, the wide dispersion in maturity and unit economics across the peer set, and the incomplete dataset on private company valuations. A credible valuation would require either expanded peer identification, direct cash-flow projections with risk-adjusted discount rates, or reliance on transaction multiples from comparable financings.
